Solution to Question 6: _—™ é #,

The fall in blood bicarbonate tevels i is cpthpeisatory ant) it it helps i increase the exercise effort, not
hinder it. ad

% mk

Adaptation at High Altitudes » a 1 fy

if 4
The work capacity is reduged_at high altitudes and this reduction is in direct proportion to the
decrease in O2 uptake... (Option B) ee

Immediate exposuré-to‘low PO2sat higher altitudes stimulates the arterial chemoreceptors, and
this stimula ‘increases alyeblar ventilation.

This immediate i increaséi pulmonary ventilation upon rising to a high altitude blows off large
quantities of CO2, reducing the PCO2 and increasing the pH of the body fluids (Respiratory
alkalosis). This raised pH inhibits the brain stem respiratory center and opposes the increased
ventilation. So, respiratory alkalosis will hinder the maximal exercise effort. (Option C).

To compensate for this respiratory alkalosis, kidneys reduce hydrogen ion secretion and increase
bicarbonate excretion. This reduces plasma and cerebrospinal fluid bicarbonate concentration and
brings the pH towards normal. This removes the inhibitory effect of alkalosis on respiration.
Hence the fall in blood bicarbonate facilitates the exercise effort.

NOTE
Lactic acid causes extreme fatigue. (Option D)

Ordinarily, the pyruvic acid from glycolysis enters the mitochondria of muscle cells and reacts
with oxygen to form many ATP molecules.

However, when there is insufficient oxygen (at high altitudes), most of the pyruvic acid then is
converted into lactic acid, which diffuses out of the muscle cells into the interstitial fluid and
blood.

812 Sold by @itachibot

======================================== PAGE 813 ========================================
Solution to Question 7:

Smoking causes no change to the sensitivity of the chemoreceptors.
Effect of Smoking on Pulmonary Ventilation in Exercise

¢ Nicotine causes constriction of the terminal bronchioles of the lungs, which increases the
resistance of air flow into and out of the lungs.

* The irritating effects of the smoke causes increased fluid secretion into the bronchial tree and
some degree of swelling of the epithelial linings, which again increases airflow resistance.

* Nicotine paralyzes the cilia on the surfaces of the respiratory epithelial cells,-Ats A result, much
debris accumulates in the passageways, and this adds further to the difficulty in breathing.

+ All these factors together make even a light smoker BB rence respiratory strain during maximal
exercise, and their level of performance may be reduced. te

* In persons with severe emphysema, as mueh, a8 four- fifths. the respirapeie ‘membrane can be
destroyed; then, even the slightest exercise can cause respiratory distress" Chronic smoking is an

important cause of emphysema. 4 co" aa

a | E
Solution to Question 8: : ty ae

‘+. my
Exercise causes an increase in n_body temperature.

Almost all the energy released by the body’s “hetabolism of nutrients is eventually converted into
body heat. Since metabdligm rises significantly during exercise there is an increase in the body
temperature. This incréase i in body temperature is significant and even lethal conditions such as
heatstroke can, éasily develop in an athlete.

ri Soe ray, Nie

Other options:

Option A: Cerebral blood flow is “autoregulated” extremely well between arterial pressure limits of
60 and 140 mm Hg. That is, the mean arterial pressure can be increased to as high as 140 mm Hg
without significant change in cerebral blood flow.

_ 4,

Option B: The arterial Po2, Pco2, and pH remain almost exactly normal even though during
strenuous exercise, O02 consumption and CO2 formation can increase as much as 20 fold.

Option C: The increase in blood flow to exercising muscles is immediate and not after 15 minutes.

Solution to Question 10:

Left shift of Hb-O2 dissociation curve is not seen during exercise.

During exercise, several factors shift the Hb-O2 dissociation curve considerably jolebe right, thus
delivering extra amounts of O2 to the active, exercising muscle fibers. o*

The right shift of Hb-O2 dissociation curve is due to the > following: a
* Release of large quantities of CO2 by the exercising, mintcles :

* Increase in the hydrogen ion concentration a the muscle caapey blood Cy

Solution to Question 11: ¢_'— _ ,
a , i

FF L a
The tremendous increase in muscle ‘blood flow that,occurs during skeletal muscle activity is
caused mainly by chemicals acting directly on the muscle arterioles to cause dilation.

These chemicals include: “= "th
* Potassium ions, a +

« Adenosine triphosphate (ATR).

* Lactic acid, andl ‘ © ie

* Carbon dioxide.

Other factors leading to increased muscle flow include
* sympathetic nervous system activation

* increase in arterial pressure, and

* increase in cardiac output.

However, the effect of local metabolites is greater than these factors in increasing the muscle
blood flow. Hence, the best answer is local metabolites.

Solution to Question 12:

There is a sharp rise in the diastolic blood pressure in isometric exercise. This is due to the
sustained compression of the blood vessels by the contracting muscles. In isotonic exercise, there

814 Sold by @itachibot

======================================== PAGE 815 ========================================
is much vasodilatation in exercising muscles. So, even though the muscles are contracting, there is
no rise in the diastolic blood pressure.

Option (a) Since the muscle pump is more active during isotonic exercise, the venous return is
higher in isotonic exercise.

Option (b) The increase in the muscle blood flow is less compared to isotonic exercise since inflow
of blood is obstructed by sustained compression exerted by the contracting muscle.

Option (d) Isometric exercise increases the after load of the heart leading to less increase in the
stroke volume of the heart.

Solution to Question 14:

During exercise, there is increased beta-adrenergic stimulation.

Medullae of the two adrenal glands secrete large amounts of norepinephrine plus epinephrine into
the circulating blood during strenuous exercise. The epinephrine often has a slight vasodilator
effect because it excites more of the beta-adrenergic receptors of the vessels, which are vasodilator
receptors, in contrast to the alpha vasoconstrictor receptors excited especially by norepinephrine.

816 Sold by @itachibot

======================================== PAGE 817 ========================================
Solution to Question 15:

Strenuous exercise can cause hyperkalemia by releasing potassium from skeletal muscle.

NOTE: Exercise leads to release of endogenous opiods and is associated with mood enhancement.
